games.name: Crocodile
games.description: Russian / Eastern European charades variant. One player picks a word and acts it out wordlessly; others guess. Wrong guesses drink. Word is usually weird, abstract, or rude.
setup: Players in a semicircle facing one player (the 'crocodile').
Pick a starter.
rules: Crocodile thinks of a word — typically weird, abstract, or hard (e.g., 'existentialism', 'butter knife', 'nostalgia').
Acts it out without speaking.
Other players guess.
Correct guess: that player becomes the next crocodile, current crocodile drinks 2.
Wrong guess: drink 1 per wrong guess.
Run out of guesses (5 minutes): everyone drinks, crocodile picks next person.
houseRules: Word categories: only verbs, only emotions, only foreign words.
Penalty escalation: 5 wrong guesses = chug.
Multilingual: words in different languages allowed.

games.name: Jackbox Party Pack Drinking
games.description: Any Jackbox game (Quiplash, Trivia Murder Party, Drawful, etc.) with drinking layered on. Last place each round drinks; winners give out drinks. Adapts to any Jackbox title.
setup: Boot up Jackbox on TV/console.
Players join via phone browsers.
Set up rules before starting.
rules: Standard Jackbox rules per game.
Drinking layers (universal):
• Last place per round: drink 3.
• First place per round: assign 5 sips.
• Lowest total at game end: chug.
Game-specific:
• Quiplash: no votes for your prompt = drink 5.
• Drawful: nobody votes for your real answer = drink 3.
• Trivia Murder Party: every wrong answer = sip.
• You Don't Know Jack: each Wrong Answer of the Game = drink 5.
houseRules: DIY rules: set them per Jackbox title.
Bonus drinks: any 'social' answer triggers everyone drinking.
Streaks: 3 wins in a row = call a permanent rule.

games.name: Picolo
games.description: Phone app that generates personalized challenges based on player names. Variable intensity (mild → wild). Auto-escalates over time. The most popular app drinking game globally.
setup: Download the Picolo app (or similar challenge-generator).
Enter all player names.
Pass the phone around in a circle.
rules: On your turn, hit 'next' on the app.
Read the prompt out loud.
Examples: 'Alex drinks if Sarah has ever been to Vegas', 'Two truths and a lie about [player]', 'Whoever last sent a text drinks'.
Perform the prompt's action.
Most prompts assign drinks based on player history or random selection.
Difficulty escalates over time as the app warms up.
houseRules: Skip-tax: skipping a prompt = drink 3.
Group vote: any prompt can be vetoed by 2+ players.
Round resets: refresh the app every 30 minutes for new prompts.

games.name: Trivia Drinking
equipment: Trivia question source (book, app, or pre-written)
games.description: Pub-style trivia with drink penalties. Wrong answer = drink. Right answer = give. Themed rounds keep it interesting. Works with Trivial Pursuit, Sporcle, or any quiz app.
setup: Pick a trivia source.
Players form teams or play solo.
Designate a question-reader.
rules: Reader poses a question.
Each player/team writes their answer.
Right: assign 2 sips to anyone.
Wrong: drink 1.
Last place per category: drink 3.
Lowest total at the end: chug.
houseRules: Multiple choice mode for casual play.
Themed rounds: 80s movies, science, etc.
Wager mode: bet sips on confidence.

games.name: Any Sports Video Game (2v2)
equipment: Sports video game (FIFA, NBA 2K, Madden, etc.)
games.description: Sports video game with drinking penalties. Each goal/score against = drink. Mercy goal? Chug. Easily layered onto any sports game; works for couch co-op too.
setup: Set up a sports game (FIFA / NBA 2K / Madden / NHL / etc.).
1v1 or 2v2.
Each player has a beer.
rules: Each goal/touchdown/point scored against you = drink 1 sip.
Special plays:
• Steal/interception: opponent drinks 2.
• Block/save (defense): drink 1 sip yourself for celebrating.
• Mercy rule (10+ run lead in your sport): losing player chugs.
Match end: loser chugs the difference (e.g., FIFA 4-1, loser chugs 3 sips).
Tournament mode: best 3 of 5; loser of series chugs full beer.
houseRules: Star player rule: if your created player scores, give 3 sips.
Penalty kicks: each PK either way = group sip.
Difficulty escalation: harder AI per win.

games.name: Icing
games.description: Late-2000s viral prank. Hide a Smirnoff Ice somewhere a friend will find it. They must drop to one knee and chug it on the spot. Iconic.
setup: Acquire Smirnoff Ice bottles.
Hide them in unexpected places where friends will find them.
rules: Discover a Smirnoff Ice presented to you (in your bag, fridge, car, etc.) = you've been Iced.
Drop to one knee and chug the entire bottle on the spot.
Counter-icing: if you have a Smirnoff Ice on your person and someone tries to Ice you, present yours — they must chug both.
Continues all night, all weekend, all year.
houseRules: Block move: only counter-Ice if you have one within 5 seconds.
Public Ice: bonus humiliation if it happens in public.
Group Ice: pre-staged at events for max chaos.

games.name: Assassin
games.description: All-night drinking assassin game. Each player draws a target. You must trick them into saying a banned word — if successful, they drink and you assassinate them, then take their target. Last assassin standing wins.
setup: Each player writes their name on a slip.
Slips shuffled; each player draws one secretly = their target.
Pick a banned word (e.g., 'right', 'tonight', any common word).
rules: Without being obvious, get your target to say the banned word.
If they say it: you've assassinated them. They drink + give you their target.
Target now becomes your new target.
Last assassin standing wins; everyone else has chugged at least once.
If you accidentally say the word yourself: drink + lose your turn for 5 minutes.
houseRules: Multiple words: 3 banned words at once.
Costume mode: assassins must point a finger gun at the target after the kill.
Innocent civilians: bystanders not in the game must announce themselves.

games.name: Wizard Staff
games.description: Tape your empty cans on top of each other to build a 'staff'. Tallest staff wins the night. The longer you can drink, the more impressive your wizardry. Famously seen in 'How I Met Your Mother'.
setup: Players each have tape and cans.
Start with 1 full can.
rules: Drink your beer.
Tape the empty can to the top of your previous can = your staff grows by 1.
Continue all night.
Tallest staff at the end = wizard.
Staff falls or breaks: forfeit + chug another can.
Standing rule: you must always carry your staff with you (or face penalty).
houseRules: Staff battles: physical battles with staffs as swords (foam or empty cans only).
Spell casting: tallest staff can 'cast' commands (you must drink, you must dance).
Trophy: photo of the winning staff for posterity.

games.name: Caps
games.description: Two-on-two beer pong precursor. Throw a bottle cap into the opposite team's cup. Make it = they drink. Cheap and minimal — good when you don't have ping pong balls.
setup: Place 1 cup at each end of a table.
Two teams of 2.
Each player has 5+ bottle caps.
rules: Take turns throwing a bottle cap at the opposite team's cup.
Cap in cup: the opposing team drinks the cup, refill.
First team to make 5 cups wins.
Loser team chugs.
houseRules: Bouncer: caps that hit and drop in count for double.
On fire: 3 in a row = unlimited shots until you miss.
Bitch cap: write 'bitch' on one cap; whoever scores it forces the other team to do a forfeit.

games.name: Left-Hand Only
games.description: Subtle ongoing rule. For the duration of the night (or game), you must drink with your non-dominant hand only. Use your dominant hand and you chug. Same idea as Buffalo but in a single-game context.
setup: Group agrees on the rule.
Specify the duration.
rules: Drink only with your non-dominant hand.
Anyone catches you using your dominant hand: chug your full drink.
Game ends when the agreed duration ends or all players have failed.
houseRules: Dominant-hand-only: reverse the rule.
Cane mode: must hold your drink with a 'cane' or umbrella.
Long-form: lifetime version (you're a buffalo for life).

games.name: Captain Paf
games.description: French verbal game. Each player has a number. The Captain says 'Paf 5!' (or any number). That player must say 'Paf 7!' or any number — but instantly. Hesitate, repeat your own number, or call yourself, and you drink.
setup: Players sit in a circle.
Number off (1, 2, 3, ...).
Pick a starter (the Captain).
rules: Captain says 'Paf [number]!' calling another player by their number.
That player MUST say 'Paf [different number]!' calling someone else within 2 seconds.
Failures:
• Hesitating > 2 seconds: drink.
• Calling your own number: drink.
• Repeating someone else's last call: drink.
• Calling a non-existent number: drink.
Game continues until everyone collapses laughing.
houseRules: Speed Paf: 1-second timer.
Renumber: every 5 minutes, swap numbers around.
Two-Paf: must say 'Paf X! Paf Y!' calling two people in one turn.

games.name: Snakes and Ladders
games.description: The childhood board game with drink stakes. Going up a ladder = give drinks based on squares climbed. Going down a snake = drink yourself based on squares fallen. The Hamming distance from start to end determines your final tally.
setup: Set up Snakes & Ladders.
Each player picks a token.
rules: Roll, move, follow ladders/snakes as normal.
Up a ladder: give drinks equal to (squares climbed) divided among other players.
Down a snake: drink (squares fallen) yourself.
First to 100: gives 10 sips. Loser: chugs.
houseRules: Doubled chutes: snakes are double penalty.
Mercy mode: split snake penalty among all players.
Bonus rolls: doubles = roll again, but drink 1 first.

games.name: Drunk Monopoly
games.description: Monopoly with drink rules per square color and event. Pay rent? Drink. Go to jail? Chug. Land on free parking? Everyone drinks. Predictably ends in chaos and ruined friendships.
setup: Standard Monopoly setup.
Drinks at the ready.
rules: Standard Monopoly rules.
Drinking layers:
• Pay rent: drink the digits of the rent amount (e.g., $200 = 2 sips).
• Buy a property: take 1 sip.
• Mortgage a property: drink 3.
• Land on free parking: everyone drinks.
• Go to jail: chug a beer.
• Pass go: give 1 drink to anyone.
• Bankrupt: chug + you're out.
houseRules: Speed Monopoly: skip auctions, fixed turn limit.
House rule generator: roll 2 dice each turn for special events.
Tax dodge: pay drinks instead of money for any tax.

games.name: Drunk Twister
games.description: Standard Twister with drink rules. Land on a circle and call your color/limb; failed pose = drink. Last person standing assigns drinks.
setup: Lay out Twister mat.
One person operates spinner.
Players stand on the mat with drinks within reach.
rules: Standard Twister: spin spinner, place limb on assigned color circle.
Drinking layers:
• Fall: drink a beer.
• Skip a turn (overwhelmed): drink 3.
• Touch the floor with anything but feet/hands during the move: drink 2.
• Cause your opponent to fall: assign 5 sips.
• Last person standing: gives out 10 sips.
houseRules: Spilled drink penalty: chug a fresh one.
Reverse Twister: avoid the assigned color.
Naked Twister: optional and at-your-own-risk.

games.name: Chickenshit
games.description: Card-based dare game where the dares involve interacting with strangers in public. Played at bars. Cards specify a dare worth a certain number of points. Highest points wins; lowest chugs.
setup: Buy or print Chickenshit deck (homemade works).
Players draw cards in turn.
Played in a public place — bar or restaurant.
rules: Each card has a dare and point value.
Examples: 'Get a stranger to compliment your outfit (5 pts)', 'Get a phone number (10 pts)', 'Buy a stranger a drink and ask if they believe in ghosts (8 pts)'.
Refuse a dare: drink the point value.
Complete the dare with witnesses: assign the points to anyone.
Most points wins; least drinks for the rest of the night.
houseRules: Self-dare: write 5 of your own dares.
Group bonus: if everyone completes a dare, double points.
Veto cards: each player gets 1 free veto.

games.name: Zoom Schwartz Profigliano
games.description: Notoriously confusing eye-contact-based verbal game. Players use 'zoom', 'schwartz', and 'profigliano' to pass attention around the circle. Mistakes happen constantly. Cult favorite among college students.
setup: Players sit in a circle.
Pick a starter.
rules: Three commands:
• 'Zoom': pass attention to anyone by making eye contact and saying 'zoom'.
• 'Schwartz': bounce attention back to the person who just zoomed you.
• 'Profigliano': skip the schwartz, attention transfers to anyone.
Add-ons (advanced): 'figliano' (next player), 'vrabajo' (everyone drinks except caller), etc.
Mistake (wrong word, wrong target, hesitate): drink.
Game ends when everyone gives up.
houseRules: Add a 4th word: 'spinkleberg' (reverse direction).
Speed-rounds: 1-second turn limit.
Sudden-death: any single mistake = chug.

games.name: Pennying
games.description: British university tradition. Drop a penny into someone's drink — they must chug it before the penny reaches the bottom. All-night, all-event mechanic. Some pubs ban it.
setup: Players carry pennies discreetly.
No formal start.
rules: Drop a penny into someone's drink while they're not looking.
If they notice: they must chug the entire drink before the penny reaches the bottom.
If they DON'T notice and drink it: they pay you a penny + a forfeit.
Counter-pennying: if you spot a penny dropping into your drink, you can hand the drink to the dropper.
Continues throughout the night.
houseRules: Coin-of-realm: only authentic pennies (no foreign coins).
Double penny: 2 pennies = chug + drink the next round.
Pennying-free zones: agree on certain spaces (the kitchen, etc.) that are pennying-safe.
